calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Wintle for and with Kim Lewis-Davis (Administrator).

Mr. Wintle provided a revised order with the correct case number. The Court noted that the estate funds were not kept in a separate interest bearing account and that mixing money in an attorney trust account is not appropriate, that there are two separate cost lists (one at the end of the time slips for ordinary services and one in the request for additional compensation) that overlap, and that the request for extraordinary fees includes time related to services provided for the period of special administration which by statute gets swept into ordinary services. The Court provided Mr. Wintle the opportunity to amend his petition to address the issues. Mr. Wintle stated that his Client does not want to amend, that he is not sure where the discrepancy is in the cost lists and that he does not want more time. The Court informed Mr. Wintle that what ever amount of interest that would have been earned on the estate account should be deducted from the fees. The Court also mentioned to Mr. Wintle that sending someone else in his place on an order to appear is not the correct response. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-10-2019 Continued To Apr-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stephen Gorman for Petitioner.

The Court noted that Mr. Wintle and his Client were ordered to be present, and expressed concern about Mr. Wintle ignoring the order to appear and Mr. Wintle representing that he filed documents when he hasn't. Mr. Gorman apologized to the Court, stated that Mr. Wintle filed a declaration on April 5th, that Mr. Wintle planned this trip from a year before, that Mr. Wintle had someone else from his office handling this and that she left in January. Mr. Gorman also stated that Mr. Wintle provided him with a copy of an amendment, that Mr. Wintle thought this would have handled things, that the copy does not show a filed stamp and that the Administrator was not aware that she was required to be here in person. The Court stated that it is inclined to suspend the Administrator and put this on for hearing as to why the Administrator should not be removed. Mr. Gorman stated that he is going to suggest to Mr. Wintle to hire someone that is familiar with the procedures of the San Francisco Probate Court. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. and the Order to Appear are continued to April 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that if the amendment is filed with courtesy copies delivered to the Court by April 17th, it may be able to go forward with this.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-13-2019 Continued To Apr-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on April 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they have failed to respond to the Probate Examiner's notes or file an amended petition. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
